---
title: "Week 8: Immersion & Operationalization"
subtitle: "Chapters 13-14"
date: "2026-10-13"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

The content analysis execution begins. Chapter 13 teaches immersion and memo writing. Chapter 14 bridges the gap between abstract concepts and measurable variables.

**Reading:** Chapter 13 and Chapter 14

## Key Concepts

- Three modes of engagement: casual, focused, analytical
- Memo writing: observational, methodological, theoretical, comparative
- Manifest vs. latent content
- The operationalization gap
- Conceptual vs. operational definitions
- NOIR levels of measurement
- Reliability and validity

## Learning Objectives

1. Immerse yourself in data using stratified sampling
2. Write observational memos documenting patterns
3. Define variables both conceptually and operationally
4. Identify the level of measurement for any variable

## Assignment: Definitions Practice (25 pts)

Define at least 4 variables with conceptual definitions, operational definitions, levels of measurement, coded values, and edge case decision rules.
